# Image Description

This sepia-toned photograph shows a horse-drawn delivery wagon parked in front of a storefront. A light-colored horse wearing harness is hitched to a wooden wagon with large spoked wheels. Two men are visible on the wagon: one seated in the front area and another standing in the back near stacked boxes and containers. The storefront behind them displays signage reading "FLAVOR & EXTRACT" and partial text mentioning "SUPPLIES" on the left side. Additional storefront windows and signage are visible in the background. Various product boxes and containers are stacked on the wagon, including what appears to be "MILK" labeling and references to "ORANGE ADE" and "SARATOGA" on the side of the wagon. The image appears to be from the early 20th century, documenting a commercial milk or beverage delivery operation.
Collection name: Jesse Brown Cook Scrapbooks Documenting San Francisco History and Law Enforcement

# Image Description

This black and white photograph shows a Parisian street scene with a large group of people gathered in the center of a cobblestone courtyard or plaza. The crowd, consisting of approximately 20-25 people wearing long coats and hats typical of mid-20th century fashion, stands beneath a black umbrella or canopy structure. On the left side of the image stands a distinguished Haussmann-style stone building featuring ornate architectural details, including large tall windows with shutters, decorative carved elements, an ornamental balcony with ironwork railing, and sculpted corbels supporting an overhanging cornice. A storefront sign reading "COIFFEUR" (hairdresser) is visible on the building's facade. To the right, neighboring buildings with simpler brick construction are visible. The courtyard is paved with regular stone or brick blocks, and additional figures can be seen on the periphery of the scene. The sky is overcast and bright, characteristic of documentary photography from this era.

# Image Description

This black and white photograph shows an urban street scene with significant construction and demolition activity. In the foreground is a dirt street with several small vendor stalls and kiosks lining the left side, including one prominently displaying a sign reading "ICE COLD DRINKS, LUNCHES, COFFEE, ICE CREAM." The centerpiece of the image is a large multi-story steel framework building under construction, with extensive scaffolding covering its upper levels and completed lower floors visible beneath. Surrounding this new construction are older, partially demolished brick buildings and industrial structures of varying heights, some appearing damaged or in states of disrepair. The buildings on the right include tall structures with grid-patterned window openings. The composition suggests this is a post-war urban redevelopment or reconstruction scene, with the contrast between the old deteriorating architecture and the new modern steel construction framework being particularly evident. At the bottom of the image is a metric ruler and color reference scale. The reference number "3:2" appears in the lower left corner of the photograph.
Collection name: 1906 San Francisco Earthquake and Fire Digital Collection

# Image Description

This is a black and white photograph depicting urban destruction and reconstruction. On the left side stands an intact neoclassical building with a distinctive domed roof, ornamental columns, and a cupola at its summit. The dome features a geometric latticed pattern. The building appears to be a civic or institutional structure, preserved amid surrounding devastation.

In stark contrast, the right side of the image shows extensive rubble, debris, and ruined brick structures. Large piles of broken concrete, bricks, and construction waste are scattered across the foreground. A partially constructed or damaged multi-story brick building with exposed framework stands on the right side of the image. Wooden scaffolding and support structures are visible among the ruins.

The street scene includes several people, a horse-drawn cart with white canvas covering, and overhead electrical or trolley lines crisscrossing the sky. A cobblestone street runs through the foreground. The photograph appears to document post-war reconstruction efforts, showing the contrast between preserved historical architecture and extensive urban damage requiring rebuilding.

At the bottom of the image is a color reference scale used for archival photography purposes.
Collection name: 1906 San Francisco Earthquake and Fire Digital Collection

# Image Description

This vintage advertising card for Sdreckels Rotisserie features a decorative ornate gold banner at the top with the establishment's name and location details: "A. Wolff prop. 15th Floor Call Bldg San Francisco." The card is divided into two main sections. On the left is a colored illustration of a tall building with a distinctive domed top labeled "CAFE," representing a San Francisco landmark. The right side depicts an interior scene of the restaurant showing two patrons - a man and woman - seated at a table set with wine bottles, glasses, and a floral centerpiece, dining while gazing out a large window at an expansive cityscape of San Francisco. Below the dining scene, text identifies the establishment as an "Ice Cream Parlor - Cafe and Restaurant" located "250 Feet Above the Bay." The card has a light cream background with brown and blue tones dominating the color palette. At the top right corner, publisher information reads "Edward H. Mitchell, Publisher, San Francisco."
Collection name: Walter Robert and Gail Lynn Kransky collection of Edward H. Mitchell postcards
